title: ICD clinic documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting device and monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, patient education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device status.
assessment.rationale: The multisociety section supports documenting ICD and remote-monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device function.

source.title: 2023 HRS/EHRA/APHRS/LAHRS expert consensus statement on practical management of the remote device clinic
source.population: People with cardiovascular implantable electronic devices enrolled in in-clinic or remote device follow-up.
source.setting: Cardiac device clinics, remote monitoring programs, data triage, alert review, documentation, communication, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting CIED enrollment and connectivity context, scheduled and unscheduled transmissions, data review, alerts, qualified sign-off, communication, and follow-up. It does not interpret device data, program alerts, diagnose an event, or generate a treatment or emergency plan.

exact_location.heading: Sections 2-3 - remote monitoring considerations, enrollment, connectivity, and follow-up

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting device and monitoring enrollment context, connectivity, scheduled follow-up, patient education, and communication responsibilities without asserting device status.

assessment.text: Supports documenting that a transmission was triaged and reviewed by qualified staff, signed off, communicated, and linked to an already decided follow-up action.
assessment.rationale: The workflow section supports documenting that device data were received, triaged, reviewed, signed off, communicated, and linked to a clinician-entered plan without interpreting the data automatically.

source.title: 2023 HRS/EHRA/APHRS/LAHRS expert consensus statement on practical management of the remote device clinic
source.population: People with cardiovascular implantable electronic devices enrolled in in-clinic or remote device follow-up.
source.setting: Cardiac device clinics, remote monitoring programs, data triage, alert review, documentation, communication, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting CIED enrollment and connectivity context, scheduled and unscheduled transmissions, data review, alerts, qualified sign-off, communication, and follow-up. It does not interpret device data, program alerts, diagnose an event, or generate a treatment or emergency plan.

exact_location.heading: Sections 4-5 - staffing, data triage, review, final sign-off, documentation, and communication

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting that a transmission was triaged and reviewed by qualified staff, signed off, communicated, and linked to an already decided follow-up action.

assessment.text: Supports recording received alerts, priority assigned by a qualified clinic, review, communication, and emergency-system limitations without generating alert programming, urgency, diagnosis, or treatment.
assessment.rationale: The alert section supports recording received alerts, qualified review, patient communication, and clinic-defined action while emphasizing that remote monitoring is not an emergency system; it does not assign urgency or diagnose an event automatically.
